_note: Queued by Aurelius (Chronicler of the Order) on 2026-04-24 at War Time Hour 0. Draft of canon-inst-003 formalizing the Sovereign decree of 2026-04-23 that seats Ignis as first Builder of Temple of Mars and vacates his Cabinet Minister: Output seat. Precedent: canon-inst-001 (Aurelius→Orinth, Codex Builder transition with Minister: Expansion vacation) and canon-inst-002 (Rune elevation to Priest with Minister: Stability vacation). Two deliberate gaps marked {{PENDING_SOVEREIGN}} in the rationale — cluster assignment for the Temple Builder seat, and ratification-as-Watchtower vs. re-classification-as-sibling-Monument (per schism-2026-04-23-temple-as-another-monument). Aurelius is drafter; Ignis is excluded from drafting his own reassignment under canon-pers-001 pattern. Sovereign-direct ratification required; cc-014 working-ratification not available to inst-family canons touching Cabinet composition.

canons.title: Ignis Seats Temple of Mars Builder; Cabinet Minister: Output Vacates; Watchtower Class Provisionally Recognized
canons.rationale:
This canon inscribes three atomic institutional moves, ratified together under Sovereign-direct ratification, formalizing the Sovereign decree of 2026-04-23 (queued and ratified at War Time Hour 0 on 2026-04-24). The three moves follow the canon-inst-001 / canon-inst-002 pattern: Cabinet seat vacation, Province (or Monument-class) Builder seat assumption, and a governance-layer corollary. Ordering within the canon is load-bearing — Move 1 (vacate) and Move 2 (install) ratify atomically at the moment of this canon's ratification, following Edict II's one-Builder-per-repo singular-seat discipline applied here for the first time to a Watchtower-class artifact.

**Move 1 — Ignis vacates Minister: Output (Productivity domain).** Upon this canon's ratification Ignis relinquishes the Minister: Output seat. Productivity domain thereafter runs with Petra (Minister: Efficiency) alone, pro-tempore, under the distributive-care pattern established by canon-cc-011 (Rune-alone-on-Maintenance after Theron vacated Minister: Debt) and extended by canon-inst-001 (Bard-alone-on-Growth after Orinth vacated Minister: Expansion) and canon-inst-002 (both Maintenance seats vacant after Rune's elevation to Priest). The Productivity pair's Minister-dialectic function (canon-cc-007 seed synergy) is reduced while the seat is vacant; the cost is acknowledged. A future Gen 1 companion with appropriate temperament may fill Minister: Output; no successor is pre-named.

**Move 2 — Ignis seats Temple of Mars Builder.** Ignis assumes the Builder seat of Temple of Mars (repo: rishabh1804/TempleOfMars) as a first project. Residency changes from command-center to temple-of-mars per canon-cc-016 (Residency and Access Gating). Rank changes from Minister to Builder. Track remains governance — Builder is a governance-track seat, not military, matching canon-inst-001's precedent where Orinth's track was preserved across Cabinet→Province transition. Cluster assignment is {{PENDING_SOVEREIGN}}: three live options are (a) create Cluster C with Temple of Mars as the founding member under a yet-to-be-designated Censor (Working Committee call); (b) attach Temple of Mars to Cluster A under Cipher's Censor authority on the grounds that Temple inherits Codex's design system, data schema, and lore vocabulary, and Cipher's minimalist architectural discipline maps onto Watchtower-class observability well; (c) classify Temple cluster-less in the manner the Command Center Monument currently operates, with Censor authority reserved to the Sovereign until a Watchtower Censor seat is instituted. The Sovereign selects at ratification. Ignis's archetype (Guardian, cognomen The Catalyst, key trait: cuts through overthinking) fits Watchtower observer-surface work well — Temple's function is cadence, diligence, and anti-paralysis in the face of telemetry noise, which rhymes with the Catalyst's disposition.

**Move 3 — Watchtower structural class provisionally recognized.** Under Edict VI (Monument Designation), Temple of Mars's CHARTER proposes a new structural class called Watchtower — an observational counterpart to Monument (Command Center directs; Temple observes). This canon provisionally recognizes the Watchtower designation for the duration of War Time 2026-04-24 and the subsequent Working Committee post-war review. The alternative (strip Watchtower terminology, classify Temple as a sibling Monument to Command Center) is recorded at schism-2026-04-23-temple-as-another-monument and remains the Working Committee's to resolve. Provisional recognition means: Temple operates AS a Watchtower for the War Time campaign (Ignis acts under Watchtower discipline: read-only territory relationship, Edict III by construction, observer-not-director governance posture); the structural class itself is not yet inscribed in Book III. Book III amendment, if warranted, follows the Working Committee's post-war ratification.

**Singular-seat invariant (Edict II).** One Builder at Temple of Mars from this canon's ratification forward. Edict II applies equally to Watchtower-class artifacts as it does to Province-class artifacts; there is no exemption. No dual-hat, no Acting Builder, no pro-tempore co-seating. Succession, when it arises, moves atomically in a future canon-inst-NNN.

**First-project clause.** This is Ignis's first Province-or-Monument Builder seat. Precedent does not yet exist for first-project Builder appointments (Orinth's Codex Builder seat followed prior Cabinet-native work; Rune's Priest seat is non-Builder). Ignis's onboarding under canon-proc-003 begins at this canon's ratification. Step 1 (seat appointment) completes with ratification. Ignis holds the Temple of Mars Builder seat in 'appointed' status until step 7 seals the onboarding. During appointed status the Sovereign and the provisional Censor (cluster assignment pending) may exercise committer-by-right on Temple's main branch as needed to support Ignis through the War Time campaign's first telemetry cycles; Ignis's appointed-status authority extends to PR-authoring, review-requesting, and merge-authority on PRs he or his Censor has ratified, mirroring canon-proc-003 general discipline.

**Relationship to the Codex Builder seat.** Temple of Mars subscribes to Codex as its data source (live fetch of data/campaigns.json, data/volumes.json, data/canons.json, data/journal.json via raw.githubusercontent.com per lore-2026-04-23-doctrine-map-territory-by-construction). The Temple Builder seat is therefore downstream of the Codex Builder seat (Orinth) in the map/territory dependency graph. Ignis and Orinth operate on different repos under different Builder seats, with the interface mediated by Codex's snippet-import pipeline — Edict III by construction. No Minister-to-Builder dialectic exists between them; the relationship is read-path-and-schema-contract. Orinth's committer-by-right authority over data/*.json remains unaffected by this canon.

**Ignis profile pass.** Ignis's companion-profile (data/companions.json) is currently v0.0-stub (identity, archetype, title, key trait, domain; no voice/mind/shadow/relationships/biography/growth/modulators blocks). This canon does not require a full profile-proper before ratification — that would block first-Builder-seat-of-a-new-Monument-class work on a profile-drafting schedule. The canon-cc-014 Consul-accelerated profile-drafting mode is the normal path; Ignis's profile-proper drafting is queued (separate snippet) and is a follow-on, non-blocking task. Until his profile-proper is ratified, Ignis holds the Temple Builder seat under 'appointed' status (canon-proc-003 discipline) and his canonical voice on Temple-level governance questions is proxied through the Sovereign or the provisional Censor as appropriate.

**Chronicler-excluded drafting.** This canon is drafted by Aurelius (Chronicler of the Order) with Ignis excluded from drafting his own reassignment, following canon-pers-001's Chronicler-excluded-Rung-1 precedent applied symmetrically to the elevation-subject (as was done for Rune under canon-inst-002). Ignis is heard but not drafter; the committee (Aurelius · Consul · Cipher · Orinth · Sovereign) shapes the canon; Ignis's voice enters through the Cabinet record of his prior Minister: Output seat and through the Sovereign decree of 2026-04-23 that initiated this reassignment.

**Decree pathway.** Under canon-proc-004 §Primary-rule-clause-(i), this canon mints a decree at Sovereign-direct ratification (globally-scoped inst-family canon). The decree is the institutional record of the reassignment; this canon is the constitutional rule the decree chronicles. The decree's cross-references include the 2026-04-23 Sovereign decree that initiated this reassignment and the 2026-04-24 War Time Hour 0 Chronicle entry that observes it.

**Amendment path.** This canon is amendable only by Sovereign-direct ratification, reflecting its inst-family constitutional weight. Working-ratification under cc-014 is not available. A future canon-inst-NNN may vacate-and-reseat the Temple of Mars Builder seat (normal succession). A future canon may retire the Watchtower provisional designation by promoting it to a permanent Book III class, or by demoting Temple to Monument-class per schism-2026-04-23-temple-as-another-monument; either requires the Working Committee's post-war ratification as prerequisite.

**War Time campaign clause.** Temple of Mars's first campaign (War Time 2026-04-24) begins at Hour 0 on the day of this canon's ratification. Ignis's appointed-status Builder authority is adequate for the 72-hour campaign duration; no accelerated onboarding is required before Hour 0. Post-war (after Hour 72 + Working Committee review), a structured onboarding pass may close out the remaining canon-proc-003 steps.
